title: Berlin eSIM, SIM & Free WiFi - Quick Summary
items: **Three real ways to get online in Berlin in 2026:** a travel eSIM bought online before the flight (Airalo, Saily, Holafly, Nomad), a German prepaid SIM/eSIM from a supermarket brand (Aldi Talk, Lidl Connect, Vodafone CallYa, Congstar), or free public WiFi (BVG, Freifunk, cafes, museums).
**For a short trip (1 to 7 days)** a travel eSIM is usually the easiest answer. Expect 4 to 18 euros for 1 to 5 GB. Set it up on your home WiFi the night before the flight.
**For 2 to 4+ weeks** a German prepaid SIM is cheaper per gigabyte and gives you a real German number. Aldi Talk and Lidl Connect are the value picks; Congstar gives the widest coverage.
**Skip the airport SIM kiosks.** They sell physical SIMs at tourist prices and rarely sell eSIMs. The same plans are 30 to 50 percent cheaper bought online or at a Berlin supermarket.

**Berlin is generous with free WiFi:** BVG-WiFi at every U-Bahn station with no registration, Freifunk in 1,000+ public spots, free WiFi on Museum Island, Brandenburger Tor area, and most cafes, museums, and hotels.
**5G is live across Telekom, Vodafone, and O2** in Berlin in 2026, and EU roaming is included by law on every German prepaid plan.
